title: At the Clinic: Describing Symptoms
learningGoal: Verbally describe medical symptoms to a doctor in Hindi.
instructions: You are feeling unwell and have gone to a local clinic. Speak into the microphone to respond to the doctor's questions. Describe your symptoms clearly using the correct vocabulary and sentence structures.
speakingPrompt: Play the role of a patient at a clinic. Answer the doctor's questions, state your symptoms (fever, headache, dizziness), and ask for medicine.
scenario: You are a tourist in India. You wake up feeling very sick. You go to a nearby clinic to see a doctor. You need to explain what is wrong with you so the doctor can prescribe the right medicine.

subTasks.prompt: Greet the doctor politely and say 'I need a doctor's help.'

sampleResponse.targetText: नमस्ते डॉक्टर साहब। मुझे आपकी मदद की ज़रूरत है।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Namaste doctor sahab. Mujhe aapki madad ki zaroorat hai.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Hello Doctor. I need your help.

subTasks.prompt: The doctor asks what happened. Tell him that you have a severe headache and fever since yesterday.

sampleResponse.targetText: मुझे बहुत तेज़ सिरदर्द है और कल से बुखार है।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Mujhe bahut tez sirdard hai aur kal se bukhar hai.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: I have a very severe headache and a fever since yesterday.

subTasks.prompt: The doctor asks if you feel anything else. Tell him that you are also feeling dizzy.

sampleResponse.targetText: हाँ, मुझे चक्कर भी आ रहे हैं।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Haan, mujhe chakkar bhi aa rahe hain.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Yes, I am also feeling dizzy.

subTasks.prompt: The doctor tells you it's due to the heat and gives you a prescription. Ask him, 'How many times a day should I take this medicine?'
expectedSpeakingPoints: Asking how many times a day

sampleResponse.targetText: मुझे यह दवा दिन में कितनी बार खानी चाहिए?
sampleResponse.transliteration: Mujhe yeh dawa din mein kitni baar khaani chahiye?
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: How many times a day should I take this medicine?

scenarioContext.targetText: डॉक्टर: नमस्ते। आइए बैठिए। बताइए, आपको क्या समस्या है?
scenarioContext.transliteration: Doctor: Namaste. Aaiye baithiye. Bataiye, aapko kya samasya hai?
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: Doctor: Hello. Come, sit. Tell me, what is your problem?

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: नमस्ते डॉक्टर साहब। मुझे आपकी मदद की ज़रूरत है।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Namaste doctor sahab. Mujhe aapki madad ki zaroorat hai.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Hello Doctor. I need your help.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: मुझे बहुत तेज़ सिरदर्द है और कल से बुखार है।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mujhe bahut tez sirdard hai aur kal se bukhar hai.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: I have a very severe headache and a fever since yesterday.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: हाँ, मुझे चक्कर भी आ रहे हैं।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Haan, mujhe chakkar bhi aa rahe hain.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yes, I am also feeling dizzy.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: मुझे यह दवा दिन में कितनी बार खानी चाहिए?
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mujhe yeh dawa din mein kitni baar khaani chahiye?
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: How many times a day should I take this medicine?

acceptableParaphrases: मेरा सिर बहुत दर्द कर रहा है (My head is hurting a lot) instead of मुझे सिरदर्द है
क्या मैं यह दवा दो बार खाऊँ? (Should I eat this medicine twice?)

criteria.name: Content
criteria.description: Did the user communicate the correct symptoms and ask the question?

criteria.name: Fluency
criteria.description: How smoothly did the user speak without long pauses?

criteria.name: Grammar
criteria.description: Was the 'Mujhe + Symptom + hai' structure used correctly?

criteria.name: Vocabulary
criteria.description: Were the correct words used for headache, fever, and dizziness?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: नमस्ते डॉक्टर साहब। मुझे आपकी मदद की ज़रूरत है। मुझे बहुत तेज़ सिरदर्द है और कल से बुखार है। हाँ, मुझे चक्कर भी आ रहे हैं। मुझे यह दवा दिन में कितनी बार खानी चाहिए?

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You communicated your symptoms clearly. A doctor would be able to treat you perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: You missed some key symptoms or the grammar was incorrect. Remember to use 'Mujhe' when describing how you feel.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Review the sample responses to improve your fluency and sentence structure.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: नमस्ते डॉक्टर साहब। मुझे आपकी मदद की ज़रूरत है... (Full response)

hintText: Use 'Mujhe' (to me) before the symptoms (e.g. Mujhe bukhar hai).
commonMistakes: Using 'Main' instead of 'Mujhe' to describe feelings.
